documentation.description:
------------------------------------------------------------------

  cargv.ads -- Create C-style "argv" vectors from strings and
              Ada.Command_Line.

  Copyright (c) 1995-2000 Terry J. Westley

  See the file "license.htm" for information on usage and
  redistribution of this file, and for a DISCLAIMER OF ALL WARRANTIES.

  This package provides the data type Chars_Ptr_Ptr which corresponds
  to the char** of C and subprograms for creating and manipulating
  arrays of C strings.

------------------------------------------------------------------

array_types.name: Vector
array_types.code: type Vector is array (CNatural range <>) of aliased C.Strings.chars_ptr;

subtypes.name: CNatural
subtypes.code: subtype CNatural is C.int range 0 .. C.int'Last;

subprograms.code: function "&" (Argv : Chars_Ptr_Ptr; Arg : String) return Chars_Ptr_Ptr

documentation.description: Construct a Chars_Ptr_Ptr using concat operation.

subprograms.name: Arg
subprograms.code: function Arg (Argv : Chars_Ptr_Ptr; N : CNatural) return String

documentation.description: Returns the Nth argument from Argv.
Returns the number of arguments in a Chars_Ptr_Ptr.

subprograms.name: Create
subprograms.code: procedure Create (Argc : out CNatural; Argv : out Chars_Ptr_Ptr)

documentation.description:
Create returns the command line arguments from Ada.Command_Line
and converts them to a C-style, null-terminated argument vector.
An empty Chars_Ptr_Ptr, used for constructors.
Free all space used by Argv.
Prints Argc and Argv to standard out.

generic_instantiations.name: Argv_Pointer
generic_instantiations.code:
package Argv_Pointer
is new C.Pointers (Index => CNatural,
                   Element => C.Strings.chars_ptr,
                   Element_Array => Vector,
                   Default_Terminator => C.Strings.Null_Ptr);
